root_cause: ZSOIL built from wrfinput ZS (soil-layer CENTERS [0.05,0.25,0.70,1.50]) instead of cumulative DZS (WRF noahmpdrv.F:689-692 -> interface depths [-0.10,-0.40,-1.00,-2.00]). Top soil DZSNSO(1) was 0.05 m instead of 0.10 m, which DOUBLED the surface ground conductance CGH=2*DF/DZSNSO(1) and shifted the whole soil column geometry; it also mis-attributed TSLB0 (init mismatch). DF itself (TDFCND) matched WRF (0.564 vs ~0.57 W/m/K).
harness_artifact_note: The localization harness grdflx_tg() passed the raw (soldn,lwdn,cosz) TUPLE to assemble_noahmp_forcing, whose rad2d('lwdn',0.0) reads .lwdn off the object -> a plain tuple has no attribute -> LWDN=0 -> one-step TG re-solve ran away cold -> bogus GRDFLX ~-200. Operationally LWDN is correct (wrapped in _NoahMPRadiation NamedTuple). GRDFLX below measured with the PROPER named-radiation wrap (real LWDN).

t2_land_bias_K.note: residual T2 cold bias is NOT a ground-flux issue: TSK matches WRF (+0.34K at 06z), GRDFLX now matches WRF. Re-localized to the surface-layer T2 skin->2m interpolation (surface_layer_diagnostics) which over-weights the cold skin in stable nocturnal conditions -- OUTSIDE this sprint's file scope.

verdict: GRDFLX over-conduction RESOLVED (collapsed ~225 -> WRF-matching 34-65 W/m2, within ~2 W/m2 every lead). TSLB0 init mismatch RESOLVED. Daytime/ocean preserved. Remaining overnight T2 bias re-localized to surface-layer T2 diagnostic (next sprint).
